content.problemStatement:
Chef is given two integers NNN and MMM. Please help Chef find any **connected undirected graph** GGG consisting of exactly NNN vertices and MMM edges, such that the number of bridges in GGG is **maximized** (among all graphs with NNN vertices and MMM edges). GGG **cannot** have self-loops or multiple edges.

If there is more than one connected undirected graph with the maximum number of bridges, you may print any one of them.

**Note:**
A bridge is an edge whose removal increases the number of connected components of the graph.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each testcase consists of a single line of input, containing two integers N,MN, MN,M - the number of vertices and edges of the graph you must construct.


Output Format
For each test case, output MMM lines where the iii-th line contains two space-separated integers denoting the iii-th edge of the graph GGG you constructed. The edges may be printed in any order, and for each edge the endpoints may be printed in any order.


Note that GGG must not contain self-loops or multiple edges, and no other graph on NNN vertices and MMM edges can contain strictly more bridges than GGG.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases do not exceed 10310^3103
Sum of MMM over all test cases do not exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is exactly 111 bridge in the graph and it cannot be increased further.



Test case 222: Since M=N‚àí1M=N-1M=N‚àí1 and we are required to output a connected graph, the graph will always be a tree and hence have 444 bridges. Any tree is acceptable here.



Test case 333: Since M=N‚ãÖ(N‚àí1)/2M= N \cdot (N-1) / 2M=N‚ãÖ(N‚àí1)/2 our only choice is a complete graph, which has 000 bridges.
